frameworks.name: theory of event coding
frameworks.one_line: action and perception share common codes via anticipated effects

frameworks.name: forward models of motor control
frameworks.one_line: motor preparation generates predictions about sensory consequences

core_question: Does action-effect anticipation bias which percept dominates in bistable perception?
key_assumptions: goal-directed action preparation activates representations of desired effects
identity predictions selectively influence subsequent perception
bistable stimuli have two competing percepts that can be biased
manipulations: congruence between anticipated action-effect and bistable percept (congruent vs incongruent)
bistable paradigm (onset binocular rivalry vs rotating-cylinder ambiguous motion)
key_findings: action-effect anticipation biased the initial percept in onset binocular rivalry
no comparable bias was observed in rotating-cylinder ambiguous motion
interpretations: forward-model predictions exert largest influence at perceptual initialization, not on continuous-resolution mechanisms
